official employment verification employee report verifier process credentialed verifiers permissible purpose

content.content:
- content: 'Northstar Analytics uses its designated external verification provider
    as

    the standard route for ordinary third-party employment verification.


    HR may issue a direct employer-authored employment verification letter only

    when the requesting organization explicitly requires evidence issued under

    the employer''s own authority and does not accept the standard verification

    provider''s output for that purpose.


    A direct letter may confirm approved employment facts such as job title,

    current employment status, and employment start date. Compensation or other

    sensitive employment information must not be added unless it is specifically

    required, authorized, and supported by the applicable process.


    This policy does not determine whether any particular recipient has the

    employer-issued-evidence requirement. That recipient requirement must be

    established separately for the current case.


    This policy also does not identify the live request destination. For a case

    that meets the condition above, use the approved HR service channel for the

    current request rather than a generic template, employee-accessible report,

    or unrelated external upload page.

    '

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Employment Data Report

  - url: https://employees.theworknumber.com/employment-data-report

  - source: The Work Number

  - domain: employees.theworknumber.com


  ## Content


  # Employment Data Report


  Employees and consumers can request and view an Employment Data Report (EDR) online
  through The Work Number employee site. The report is intended to help you understand
  the employment and income information associated with your file in The Work Number
  database.


  The report can show employment-related data connected to your record and information
  about organizations that have obtained information from your file. It is designed
  for you to review your own data and understand how your file is represented in the
  system.


  ## Important purpose limitation


  Your Employment Data Report is not an employment verification. It is prepared for
  your own informational purposes. The report should not be treated as a verifier-issued
  report for another organization, and it is not intended for another person or organization
  to access as though it were their formal verification result.


  The employee report and the verifier process serve different purposes. Employees
  use the report to see and review information about themselves. Organizations seeking
  to verify someone''s employment or income use a separate verification process designed
  for credentialed verifiers.


  ## Using your report


  Use the employee site to access your report and review the information associated
  with your file. Check the information carefully, including the employment data shown
  and the record of access to your information.


  If you believe information in your file is inaccurate or incomplete, the employee
  site provides a separate process for disputing employment data. Privacy controls
  are also available through the employee site, including the option to place a data
  freeze that can limit access by many verifiers.


  The Employment Data Report is therefore a personal access and review tool. It helps
  you inspect your own file, but it does not replace the formal process used by an
  organization that needs an authorized employment or income verification.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Employment Data Report

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How It Works

  - url: https://theworknumber.com/how-it-works

  - source: The Work Number

  - domain: theworknumber.com


  ## Content


  # How It Works


  The Work Number is designed to support employment and income verification between
  consumers and credentialed verifiers. The service helps eligible organizations obtain
  employment or income information without relying only on documents supplied by the
  consumer.


  ## Verification access


  Credentialed verifiers with a permissible purpose can access important employment
  and income data through The Work Number. The verification process is intended for
  organizations that have a valid reason to obtain the information and that meet the
  service''s access requirements.


  Depending on the use case, verifiers may include organizations involved in lending,
  housing, employment screening, government programs, or other authorized decisions.
  The verifier uses the service for the applicable verification purpose rather than
  asking the employee to provide their personal Employment Data Report as a substitute
  for the verifier''s own authorized result.


  ## Direct verification workflow


  The service is built to reduce delays caused by waiting for consumers to gather
  and send documents. When the necessary employment data is available, an authorized
  verifier can obtain information through the verifier-facing process.


  The verification relationship has distinct roles:


  - the consumer is the person whose employment or income information is being considered;

  - the employer or payroll data source supplies employment information to the system;

  - the credentialed verifier accesses information for a permissible purpose through
  the verification service.


  The consumer-facing employee site and the verifier-facing process are not interchangeable.
  Employees can review and manage information about themselves through the employee
  experience, while organizations seeking a formal verification use the verifier service
  appropriate to their purpose.


  ## Start with the correct verification use case


  Organizations should use the verification path that matches their purpose. The Work
  Number offers employment and income verification services for business and organizational
  users, as well as separate experiences for government-program verification and other
  permissible purposes.


  A formal verification should therefore be obtained through the appropriate verifier
  process. Access to employee-facing information does not, by itself, turn a personal
  report or a separate upload page into the formal result produced for a credentialed
  verifier.

  '
